NMS typically develops within the first few weeks of starting the medication or with a dose increase.

The symptoms include hyperthermia, muscle rigidity (especially lead-pipe rigidity), altered mental status like confusion or delirium, autonomic instability (tachycardia, hypertension), and elevated creatine kinase due to muscle breakdown.
Other possibilities might include serotonin syndrome, but that usually has more agitation, diarrhea, myoclonus, and clonus.
The rigidity in NMS is more pronounced and different from the tremors in serotonin syndrome.
